title: Parallax Write Instruction (m-unit-work)
description: The canonical, language-neutral write-instruction vocabulary a unit of work buffers and flushes (m-unit-work) â€” the write-side analogue of `operation.schema.json`. A write from any frontend lowers into exactly one keyed or predicate instruction. Valid-Time input is spelled `validFrom`; a bounded `*Until` operation additionally carries `until`, so its window is `[validFrom, until)`. The finite Transaction-Time instant is Clock context supplied at flush and is not an instruction member. The framework-owned optimistic observation is likewise flush-time context: `observedVersion` and `observedTxStart` are forbidden on durable rows. There are no business/processing aliases. SERDE ROUND-TRIP CONTRACT: a conforming implementation MUST round-trip every instruction through this canonical form losslessly â€” `serialize(deserialize(x)) == x` (JSON and YAML).

keyedWriteInstruction.description: A keyed write: a mutation on one entity carrying flat attribute-named rows. A Valid-Time mutation carries `validFrom`; the bounded `*Until` forms additionally carry `until`.

rows.description: The neutral write input: one or more flat attribute-named rows the mutation operates on (`$defs/writeRow`). A generating implementation classifies each field by its metamodel role and derives the emitted column list and ORDER from `columnOrder(entity)`.

validFrom.description: The Valid-Time lower bound of the mutation (`from_z`). Required on every bounded `*Until` mutation.

until.description: The exclusive Valid-Time upper bound of a bounded `*Until` mutation (`thru_z`), always paired with `validFrom`.

allOf.description: A bounded `*Until` mutation is over `[validFrom, until)`, so it must carry both Valid-Time bounds; a plain mutation must omit `until`.

predicateWriteInstruction.description: A predicate-selected (set-based) write: a mutation on every row of `target.entity` matching `target.predicate`, with `assignments` on the update forms. This is the set-based analogue of the keyed instruction; `m-batch-write` / `m-opt-lock` decide whether it lowers readless (unversioned non-temporal) or materializes per-row (versioned / temporal). Assignment list order is DATA order only â€” descriptor `columnOrder(entity)` determines the emitted SET columns and their binds.

validFrom.description: The Valid-Time lower bound for a plain or bounded temporal predicate write (`from_z`). Required on every bounded `*Until` write.

until.description: The exclusive Valid-Time upper bound of a bounded predicate write (`thru_z`), always paired with `validFrom`.

allOf.description: The assignment-bearing update forms (`update` / `updateUntil`) MUST carry `assignments`; `delete` / `terminate` / `terminateUntil` MUST NOT.
A bounded predicate write is over `[validFrom, until)`, so it must carry both Valid-Time bounds; unbounded forms must omit `until`.

writeTarget.description: The exact entity a predicate-selected write begins from plus its bare `m-op-algebra` predicate. The predicate is validated against `operation.schema.json` and the descriptor-dependent scope / bare-predicate rules separately (it must be a bare write predicate, never a result modifier).

writeAssignment.description: One ordered predicate-write assignment. `attr` is a qualified descriptor attribute or top-level value-object reference and `value` is a neutral literal: a scalar / null for an attribute or the complete object / array document for a value object. Duplicate and unassignable (framework-owned / PK / relationship) references are rejected by model-aware validation.

writeRow.description: A flat attribute-named durable write row. Every key is an Entity Attribute or top-level Value Object name, never a physical column. The framework-owned optimistic observations `observedVersion` and `observedTxStart` are attached only at flush and are forbidden here. Scalar fields carry neutral literals or DB-computed markers; Value Object fields carry their complete ordered document value.

not.description: The framework-owned optimistic observation is not durable instruction state: neither `observedVersion` nor the observed Transaction-Time start (`observedTxStart`) may ride on a durable row.

writeRowValue.description: The value a neutral write-row field carries, classified by the field's metamodel role (resolved from `columnOrder(entity)`, NOT from the value's shape): a scalar literal / explicit `null` for a scalar attribute; a one-key DB-computed marker (`writeComputedMarker`) for a computed scalar column; or the WHOLE embedded value-object document â€” a JSON object for a `one` member, a JSON array of objects for a `many` member â€” bound atomically in its columnOrder position. The branches are NON-exclusive (`anyOf`): a marker-shaped value-object document validates via both the marker and the document branch, and the model role decides. This is the single shared value vocabulary `compatibility-case.schema.json`'s flush-context write row references rather than redefining.

anyOf.description: A value-object document (m-value-object): a value-object-named field binds the WHOLE embedded composite atomically as one document value in its columnOrder position â€” a JSON object for a `one` value object, a JSON array of objects for a `many` one.

writeComputedMarker.description: A one-key DB-COMPUTED marker in a neutral write row: the attribute's value is derived by the database rather than authored as a literal bind, so the generating implementation emits the strategy's SQL fragment. Applies ONLY to a scalar-attribute column (a value-object column's value is always the literal document even when marker-shaped, disambiguated by metamodel role, not by shape).

computed.description: The pk-gen `max` strategy: the column is emitted as `coalesce(max(col), ?) + ?` â€” the computation folds into the INSERT's SQL, so a `max`-strategy insert stays statically derivable.

increment.description: A self-referential advance emitted as `col = col + ?` (e.g. a sequence registry's `next_val`); the integer is the amount added.

instant.description: A Valid-Time instant: an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp string, or the open-bound sentinel `infinity`.

entityName.description: An exact concrete descriptor entity name (the position a write begins from).

keyedMutation.description: The keyed write mutation surface: the MVP non-temporal / audit-only verbs (`insert` / `update` / `delete` / `terminate`) plus the full-bitemporal bounded rectangle split (`insertUntil` / `updateUntil` / `terminateUntil`). Deferred extension verbs are added here when their slices claim them. `compatibility-case.schema.json`'s buffered keyed write references this enum rather than redefining it.

predicateMutation.description: The predicate-selected (set-based) write mutation surface: the update / delete / terminate verbs plus the bounded `updateUntil` / `terminateUntil` (a set-based write has no `insert` form â€” there is no predicate to select rows that do not yet exist). `compatibility-case.schema.json`'s `predicateWrite` references this enum rather than redefining it.
